verse_specific_anchors: the nine-property structure — four restated denials (uncleavable/incombustible/unwettable/undryable) plus five additions (eternal/all-pervading/stable/immovable/primordial), each individually mapped to a distinct engineering property
the sthāṇu/acala preservation — the analog distinguishes 'does not drift' from 'versioning by addition not mutation' rather than collapsing them

the fintech audit scenario tests each property against named contracts (/v1/charge passes, /v1/balance fails two specific properties, /admin/internal/* fails all-pervading) — the nine-property audit format is 2.24-specific
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 2.23 (the four denials only — overlaps but lacks the five additions), BG 2.20 (the soul as nityaḥ ajaḥ — overlaps the eternal/unborn pair but lacks the nine-property exhaustive list), BG 13.13-17 (the field-knower's properties — different chapter, different list). None has the exhaustive nine-property structure of 2.24. The analog is anchored on 2.24-specific completeness. PASS — not transferable.
